title: Modern Media Console with Boxed Base
summary: A plywood media console with a divided cabinet and a slim rectangular-tube base — built here with a wood box-frame base as a genuine substitute for the original welded-metal version.
description:
The original version of this design stands on welded 3/4" square steel tubing, which gives it a very thin, almost floating-looking base — a look this catalog's tool list can't reproduce, since welding isn't among the tools covered here. The source itself offers the fix: the same base can be built from wood, using 2x2s or hardwood 1x1 stock in place of the tubing, and that's the version authored here. The plywood carcass on top — sides, shelf, divider, hinged door — is identical either way.

If you do have welding equipment and want the original metal-base look, the plywood case above still applies unchanged; only the base construction differs. For the wood base shown here, keep the frame's proportions slim (1x1 or 2x2, not thicker) to preserve the light, low-profile stance that's the point of this design — a chunky base would fight the whole aesthetic.

tools.note: For ripping the plywood into clean, consistent strips.

materials.name: Pine or hardwood, 2x2 or 1x1, 24 ft total
materials.note: Wood base substitute for the original welded 3/4" square steel tubing.

steps.title: Build the box frame
steps.body: Build the basic box frame for the console using the plywood sides, shelves, and divider. Use a pocket hole jig with the 3/4" setting and 1-1/4" pocket hole screws — with no face frame to reinforce these joints, use three pocket holes per joint for this width of board.

steps.title: Add the shelves
steps.body: Add shelves as desired with pocket holes. A shelf-pin jig lets you make the shelves adjustable instead, if preferred.

steps.title: Attach the back
steps.body: Attach the back panel with glue and 3/4" brad nails or staples, securing it to every fixed shelf as well as the sides.

steps.title: Hinge the door
steps.body: Hang the door with your chosen hinges. Joining a few 1x boards together with a pocket hole jig is a good way to build a solid door if you don't want to use a single plywood panel.

steps.title: Build the wood-frame base
steps.body: Build a slim rectangular frame from 2x2 or hardwood 1x1 stock as a substitute for the original design's welded steel tubing — keep the stock thin to preserve the low, light stance that's the whole point of this design. Join the frame with glue and screws, checking for square.
materials: Pine or hardwood, 2x2 or 1x1, 24 ft total

steps.title: Attach the console to the base
steps.body: Attach the console to the underside of the base frame with 1-1/4" screws — pocket hole screws work fine for this. Sand every surface and finish.

images.alt: Modern media console with a slim box-frame base
